documentation.value:
Write arguments to stdout.

```bash
echo "Hello, world!"
echo -n "No newline"
echo -e "Tab:\tNewline:\n"
```

Formats and prints arguments under control of a format string.

```bash
printf "Name: %s, Age: %d\n" "Alice" 30
printf "%05d\n" 42
printf -v var "formatted %s" "string"
```

Read a line from standard input and split it into fields.

```bash
read -p "Enter name: " name
read -r line
read -a array_var
read -t 5 -p "Timeout in 5s: " answer
```

Change the shell working directory.

```bash
cd /path/to/dir
cd ~
cd -        # go to previous directory
cd ..       # go up one level
```

Print the name of the current working directory.

```bash
pwd
pwd -P   # resolve symlinks
pwd -L   # use logical path (default)
```

Mark a variable for export to child processes.

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"
export MY_VAR="value"
export -n MY_VAR    # unexport
export -p           # list all exported
```

completions.label: source
completions.detail: Execute commands from a file in the current shell

documentation.value:
Read and execute commands from a file in the current shell environment.

```bash
source ~/.bashrc
source ./config.sh
. ./env.sh        # shorthand for source
```

Create a shortcut name for a command.

```bash
alias ll='ls -la'
alias gs='git status'
alias       # list all aliases
alias ll    # show specific alias
```

Remove alias definitions.

```bash
unalias ll
unalias -a    # remove all aliases
```

Indicate how a command name would be interpreted.

```bash
type ls       # ls is aliased to ...
type cd       # cd is a shell builtin
type -t echo  # builtin
type -a echo  # show all locations
```

Show the full path of a command.

```bash
which python
which -a node   # show all matches
```

completions.label: eval
completions.detail: Evaluate arguments as a shell command

documentation.value:
Concatenate arguments and execute them as a shell command.

```bash
cmd="ls -la"
eval "$cmd"
eval "echo \$HOME"
```

completions.label: exec
completions.detail: Replace the shell with a command

documentation.value:
Replace the shell process with the specified command, or redirect file descriptors.

```bash
exec /bin/zsh
exec > output.log 2>&1    # redirect all output
exec 3< input.txt         # open fd 3 for reading
```

Exit the shell with a status code.

```bash
exit 0    # success
exit 1    # general error
exit      # exit with last command's status
```

Return from a shell function with a status code.

```bash
my_func() {
    if [[ -z "$1" ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}
```

Shift positional parameters to the left by n positions (default 1).

```bash
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    echo "$1"
    shift
done
shift 2   # shift by 2 positions
```

Execute a command when the shell receives a signal.

```bash
trap 'echo Interrupted' INT
trap 'rm -f /tmp/tmpfile; exit' EXIT
trap '' SIGTERM     # ignore SIGTERM
trap - INT          # reset to default
```

Set shell options and positional parameters.

```bash
set -e          # exit on error
set -u          # error on unset variables
set -o pipefail # fail on pipe errors
set -x          # print commands before execution
set -- "a" "b"  # set positional params
```

Remove variable or function definitions.

```bash
unset MY_VAR
unset -f my_function
unset -v my_variable
```

Declare variables and set attributes.

```bash
declare -i num=42       # integer
declare -r CONST="val"  # readonly
declare -a arr=(1 2 3)  # indexed array
declare -A map          # associative array
declare -x VAR="val"    # export
declare -l lower="ABC"  # lowercase
declare -u upper="abc"  # uppercase
```

Create a local variable visible only within a function.

```bash
my_func() {
    local name="Alice"
    local -i count=0
    local -a items=(a b c)
}
```

Mark variables or functions as readonly.

```bash
readonly PI=3.14159
readonly -a COLORS=(red green blue)
readonly -f my_func
```

Execute commands based on a condition.

```bash
if [[ condition ]]; then
    commands
elif [[ condition ]]; then
    commands
else
    commands
fi
```
Begins the body of an `if` or `elif` conditional block.

Additional condition check within an `if` block.

```bash
if [[ $x -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo "one"
elif [[ $x -eq 2 ]]; then
    echo "two"
fi
```
Default branch when no `if`/`elif` conditions are true.
Closes an `if` conditional block.

Iterate over a list of items.

```bash
for item in "${list[@]}"; do
    echo "$item"
done

for i in {1..10}; do
    echo "$i"
done

for ((i=0; i<10; i++)); do
    echo "$i"
done
```

Execute commands while a condition is true.

```bash
while [[ condition ]]; do
    commands
done

while read -r line; do
    echo "$line"
done < file.txt
```

Execute commands until a condition becomes true.

```bash
until [[ $count -ge 10 ]]; do
    ((count++))
done
```
Marks the beginning of the loop body in `for`, `while`, `until`, and `select` loops.
Marks the end of a `for`, `while`, `until`, or `select` loop.
Closes a `case` pattern matching block.

Exit from a `for`, `while`, `until`, or `select` loop.

```bash
break      # break innermost loop
break 2    # break out of 2 nested loops
```

Resume the next iteration of a loop.

```bash
continue     # skip to next iteration
continue 2   # continue outer loop
```

Search for lines matching a pattern.

```bash
grep "pattern" file.txt
grep -r "TODO" ./src
grep -i -n "error" log.txt
grep -E "^[0-9]+" data.txt
grep -v "exclude" file.txt
grep -c "count" file.txt
```

Transform text using patterns and commands.

```bash
sed 's/old/new/g' file.txt
sed -i 's/old/new/g' file.txt       # in-place edit
sed -n '10,20p' file.txt             # print lines 10-20
sed '/pattern/d' file.txt            # delete matching lines
sed '1i\Header line' file.txt       # insert at line 1
```

Process and analyze text files.

```bash
awk '{print $1}' file.txt
awk -F: '{print $1, $3}' /etc/passwd
awk '/pattern/ {print $0}' file.txt
awk 'NR==1 {print}' file.txt
awk '{sum += $1} END {print sum}' data.txt
```

completions.label: find
completions.detail: Search for files in a directory hierarchy

documentation.value:
Find files and directories matching criteria.

```bash
find . -name "*.txt"
find . -type f -name "*.log" -mtime -7
find /path -size +100M
find . -name "*.tmp" -delete
find . -type f -exec chmod 644 {} \;
find . -name "*.py" | xargs grep "import"
```

completions.label: xargs
completions.detail: Build and execute commands from stdin

documentation.value:
Construct argument lists and execute commands.

```bash
find . -name "*.txt" | xargs grep "pattern"
echo "a b c" | xargs -n1 echo
find . -name "*.bak" -print0 | xargs -0 rm
cat urls.txt | xargs -P 4 -I {} curl {}
```

Sort lines in text files.

```bash
sort file.txt
sort -n numbers.txt       # numeric sort
sort -r file.txt          # reverse sort
sort -u file.txt          # unique sort
sort -t: -k3 -n /etc/passwd
```

Filter adjacent matching lines.

```bash
sort file.txt | uniq
sort file.txt | uniq -c    # count occurrences
sort file.txt | uniq -d    # only duplicates
sort file.txt | uniq -u    # only unique
```

completions.label: wc
completions.detail: Word, line, character, and byte count

documentation.value:
Count lines, words, and characters.

```bash
wc file.txt         # lines, words, chars
wc -l file.txt      # line count only
wc -w file.txt      # word count only
wc -c file.txt      # byte count
find . -name '*.py' | wc -l
```

Extract columns or fields from text.

```bash
cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd    # first field, colon delim
cut -c1-10 file.txt         # characters 1-10
cut -f2,4 -d'\t' data.tsv  # fields 2 and 4
```

Translate, squeeze, or delete characters.

```bash
echo "HELLO" | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z'    # to lowercase
echo "hello" | tr 'a-z' 'A-Z'    # to uppercase
tr -d '\n' < file.txt             # delete newlines
tr -s ' ' < file.txt              # squeeze spaces
```

completions.label: tee
completions.detail: Read from stdin and write to stdout and files

documentation.value:
Duplicate output to both stdout and files.

```bash
command | tee output.log
command | tee -a output.log     # append
command | tee file1 file2       # multiple files
```

completions.label: head
completions.detail: Output the first part of files

documentation.value:
Display the first lines of a file.

```bash
head file.txt          # first 10 lines
head -n 20 file.txt    # first 20 lines
head -c 100 file.txt   # first 100 bytes
```

completions.label: tail
completions.detail: Output the last part of files

documentation.value:
Display the last lines of a file.

```bash
tail file.txt          # last 10 lines
tail -n 20 file.txt    # last 20 lines
tail -f /var/log/syslog # follow log in real time
tail -n +5 file.txt    # from line 5 onwards
```

Concatenate files and print to stdout.

```bash
cat file.txt
cat file1.txt file2.txt > combined.txt
cat -n file.txt        # with line numbers
cat -A file.txt        # show all (tabs, EOL)
```

View file contents page by page.

```bash
less file.txt
less -N file.txt       # with line numbers
less +F /var/log/syslog # follow mode
```

Compare files and show differences.

```bash
diff file1.txt file2.txt
diff -u old.txt new.txt    # unified format
diff -r dir1/ dir2/        # recursive compare
diff --color file1 file2
```

Change file mode bits.

```bash
chmod +x script.sh
chmod 755 script.sh
chmod -R 644 ./dir
chmod u+rw,go-w file.txt
```

Change file ownership.

```bash
chown user:group file.txt
chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www
chown user file.txt
```

Create one or more directories.

```bash
mkdir mydir
mkdir -p path/to/nested/dir
mkdir -m 755 secure_dir
```

Remove empty directories.

```bash
rmdir empty_dir
rmdir -p path/to/empty_dirs
```

Copy files and directories.

```bash
cp source.txt dest.txt
cp -r source_dir/ dest_dir/
cp -a source/ dest/          # preserve attributes
cp -i file.txt backup/       # interactive (prompt)
```

Move or rename files and directories.

```bash
mv old.txt new.txt
mv file.txt /path/to/dest/
mv -i source dest       # interactive
mv -n source dest       # no overwrite
```

Remove files or directories.

```bash
rm file.txt
rm -f file.txt          # force, no prompt
rm -r directory/         # recursive
rm -rf directory/        # force recursive
rm -i file.txt           # interactive
```

Create hard and symbolic links.

```bash
ln -s /path/to/target link_name   # symbolic link
ln target link_name                # hard link
ln -sf /new/target link_name      # force symbolic
```

Create an empty file or update file timestamps.

```bash
touch newfile.txt
touch -t 202301010000 file.txt   # set specific time
touch -r ref_file target_file    # copy timestamp
```

Create and extract archive files.

```bash
tar czf archive.tar.gz dir/    # create gzipped
tar xzf archive.tar.gz         # extract gzipped
tar xjf archive.tar.bz2        # extract bzip2
tar tf archive.tar              # list contents
tar czf backup.tar.gz --exclude='*.log' dir/
```

Compress or expand files.

```bash
gzip file.txt           # compress (replaces file)
gzip -d file.txt.gz     # decompress
gzip -k file.txt        # keep original
gzip -9 file.txt        # max compression
```

completions.label: curl
completions.detail: Transfer data from or to a server

documentation.value:
Make HTTP requests and transfer data.

```bash
curl https://example.com
curl -o file.txt https://example.com/file
curl -X POST -d '{"key":"val"}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' url
curl -sS https://example.com | jq .
curl -L -O https://example.com/download.tar.gz
```

Non-interactive network downloader.

```bash
wget https://example.com/file.tar.gz
wget -O output.html https://example.com
wget -q -O- https://example.com
wget -r -np https://example.com/dir/
```

Evaluate conditional expressions. Usually used as `[` or `[[`.

```bash
test -f file.txt && echo "exists"
[ -d /path ] && echo "is directory"
[[ -z "$var" ]] && echo "empty"
[[ "$a" == "$b" ]] && echo "equal"
```

completions.label: [[ -f file ]]
completions.detail: Test if file exists and is regular file

documentation.value:
Conditional test operators:

```bash
[[ -f file ]]    # file exists and is regular file
[[ -d dir ]]     # directory exists
[[ -e path ]]    # path exists (any type)
[[ -r file ]]    # file is readable
[[ -w file ]]    # file is writable
[[ -x file ]]    # file is executable
[[ -s file ]]    # file exists and is non-empty
[[ -L file ]]    # file is symbolic link
```

Test if path is a directory.

```bash
if [[ -d "$dir" ]]; then
    echo "Directory exists"
fi
```

Test if a file or directory exists.

```bash
if [[ -e "$path" ]]; then
    echo "Path exists"
fi
```

Test if a string has zero length.

```bash
if [[ -z "$var" ]]; then
    echo "Variable is empty"
fi
```

Test if a string has non-zero length.

```bash
if [[ -n "$var" ]]; then
    echo "Variable is not empty"
fi
```

Integer comparison operators:

```bash
[[ $a -eq $b ]]    # equal
[[ $a -ne $b ]]    # not equal
[[ $a -gt $b ]]    # greater than
[[ $a -lt $b ]]    # less than
[[ $a -ge $b ]]    # greater or equal
[[ $a -le $b ]]    # less or equal
```

Use default value if variable is unset or null.

```bash
name=${name:-"World"}
echo "Hello, ${user:-guest}"
```

Use alternate value if variable IS set and non-null.

```bash
result=${var:+"var is set"}
flags="${verbose:+-v}"
```

Get the length of a string variable.

```bash
str="Hello"
echo ${#str}    # 5
echo ${#arr[@]} # array length
```

Remove shortest trailing pattern match.

```bash
path="/home/user/file.txt"
echo ${path%/*}       # /home/user
echo ${path%.txt}     # /home/user/file
```

Remove longest trailing pattern match.

```bash
path="dir1/dir2/file.tar.gz"
echo ${path%%/*}      # dir1
echo ${path%%.*}      # dir1/dir2/file
```

Remove shortest leading pattern match.

```bash
path="/home/user/file.txt"
echo ${path#*/}       # home/user/file.txt
```

Remove longest leading pattern match.

```bash
path="/home/user/file.txt"
echo ${path##*/}      # file.txt
```

Replace first match of pattern.

```bash
str="hello world hello"
echo ${str/hello/Hi}      # Hi world hello
```

Replace all matches of pattern.

```bash
str="hello world hello"
echo ${str//hello/Hi}      # Hi world Hi
```

Extract a substring.

```bash
str="Hello World"
echo ${str:0:5}    # Hello
echo ${str:6}      # World
echo ${str: -5}    # World (note the space)
```

Convert first character to uppercase.

```bash
name="alice"
echo ${name^}     # Alice
echo ${name^^}    # ALICE (all uppercase)
```

Convert first character to lowercase.

```bash
name="ALICE"
echo ${name,}     # aLICE
echo ${name,,}    # alice (all lowercase)
```

Complete if-then-fi conditional block.

```bash
if [[ condition ]]; then
    commands
fi
```

completions.label: if-then-else-fi

documentation.value:
Complete if-then-else-fi block.

```bash
if [[ condition ]]; then
    commands
else
    other_commands
fi
```

For-in loop over a list.

```bash
for item in "${array[@]}"; do
    echo "$item"
done
```

C-style for loop.

```bash
for ((i=0; i<10; i++)); do
    echo "$i"
done
```

Read a file line by line.

```bash
while IFS= read -r line; do
    echo "$line"
done < file.txt
```

Infinite loop until break.

```bash
while true; do
    commands
    [[ condition ]] && break
done
```

Define a new shell function.

```bash
my_function() {
    local arg1="$1"
    # function body
}
```

completions.label: #!/bin/bash

documentation.value:
Shebang line for a bash script with strict mode.

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
```

completions.label: #!/usr/bin/env bash

documentation.value:
Portable shebang line using env to find bash.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
```

Multi-line string using a here document.

```bash
cat <<EOF
Hello, $USER.
Today is $(date).
EOF

cat <<'EOF'   # no variable expansion
$literal \text
EOF
```

Feed a string to a command's stdin.

```bash
read -r first rest <<< "Hello World"
grep "pattern" <<< "$string_var"
```

Set up a cleanup function that runs on script exit.

```bash
cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$tmpdir"
}
trap cleanup EXIT
```

completions.label: getopts-loop

documentation.value:
Loop over all elements of an array.

```bash
for item in "${array[@]}"; do
    echo "$item"
done
```

completions.label: pipe-while
completions.detail: Process command output line by line (snippet)

documentation.value:
Pipe command output to a while-read loop.

```bash
command | while IFS= read -r line; do
    echo "$line"
done
```

completions.insertText:
${1:command} | while IFS= read -r ${2:line}; do
	echo "\$${2:line}"
done

documentation.value:
Use command output as a file.

```bash
diff <(command1) <(command2)
while read -r line; do
    echo "$line"
done < <(command)
```

Capture command output into a variable.

```bash
result=$(command arg1 arg2)
today=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
count=$(wc -l < file.txt)
```

Perform arithmetic.

```bash
((count++))
((result = a + b))
if ((x > 10)); then echo "big"; fi
result=$((a * b + c))
```

Safely create and clean up a temporary file.

```bash
tmpfile=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$tmpfile"' EXIT
```

Safely create and clean up a temporary directory.

```bash
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
```

completions.label: check-command-exists

documentation.value:
Verify a command is available before using it.

```bash
if ! command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
    echo "docker not found"
    exit 1
fi
```

completions.insertText:
if ! command -v ${1:command} &> /dev/null; then
	echo "${1:command} is not installed" >&2
	exit 1
fi

documentation.value:
Define an error handler with line number reporting.

```bash
die() {
    echo "Error: $*" >&2
    exit 1
}
```

completions.label: logging

documentation.value:
Reliably get the directory of the current script.

```bash
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
```

Ask for user confirmation.

```bash
read -rp "Are you sure? [y/N] " ans
[[ "$ans" =~ ^[Yy] ]] || exit 0
```

completions.label: retry-loop

documentation.value:
Retry a command with exponential backoff.

```bash
retry() {
    local max=5 delay=1 i
    for ((i=1; i<=max; i++)); do
        "$@" && return 0
        sleep $delay
        ((delay *= 2))
    done
    return 1
}
```

completions.insertText:
retry() {
	local max=${1:5} delay=1 i
	for ((i=1; i<=max; i++)); do
		"\$@" && return 0
		echo "Attempt \$i/\$max failed. Retrying in \${delay}s..." >&2
		sleep \$delay
		((delay *= 2))
	done
	return 1
}

completions.label: parallel-jobs

documentation.value:
Run commands in parallel and wait for all.

```bash
pids=()
for item in "${items[@]}"; do
    process "$item" &
    pids+=("$!")
done
for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    wait "$pid"
done
```

completions.insertText:
pids=()
for ${1:item} in "${${2:items}[@]}"; do
	${3:process} "\$${1:item}" &
	pids+=("\$!")
done
for pid in "\${pids[@]}"; do
	wait "\$pid"
done

documentation.value:
Return the filename portion of a path.

```bash
basename /path/to/file.txt        # file.txt
basename /path/to/file.txt .txt   # file
basename -a /path/a /path/b       # a  b
```

Return the directory portion of a path.

```bash
dirname /path/to/file.txt    # /path/to
dirname ./file.txt            # .
```

completions.label: date
completions.detail: Display or set date and time

documentation.value:
Display the current date/time or convert date formats.

```bash
date
date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
date -d "yesterday" +%Y-%m-%d
date -u    # UTC time
```

Pause execution for a specified duration.

```bash
sleep 5      # 5 seconds
sleep 0.5    # half second
sleep 1m     # 1 minute
sleep 1h     # 1 hour
```

Safely create a temporary file or directory.

```bash
tmpfile=$(mktemp)
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
mktemp /tmp/myapp.XXXXXX
```

completions.label: xargs
completions.detail: Build and execute commands from stdin

documentation.value:
Construct argument lists and invoke commands.

```bash
find . -name '*.txt' | xargs wc -l
echo '1 2 3' | xargs -n1 echo
find . -print0 | xargs -0 rm
cat list.txt | xargs -I {} cp {} /dest/
```

Wait for background jobs to complete.

```bash
command1 &
command2 &
wait         # wait for all
wait $pid    # wait for specific PID
wait -n      # wait for any one job
```

Display status of jobs in the current session.

```bash
jobs
jobs -l   # include PID
jobs -p   # PIDs only
```

Resume a suspended job in the background.

```bash
bg       # resume last stopped job
bg %1    # resume job 1
```

Bring a background job to the foreground.

```bash
fg       # bring last job to foreground
fg %1    # bring job 1 to foreground
```

Send a signal to a process.

```bash
kill $pid
kill -9 $pid       # SIGKILL (force)
kill -TERM $pid    # SIGTERM (graceful)
kill -HUP $pid     # SIGHUP (reload)
kill %1            # kill job 1
```

Save current directory and change to a new one.

```bash
pushd /some/dir     # save cwd, cd to /some/dir
popd                # return to saved directory
```

Return to the directory saved by pushd.

```bash
pushd /tmp
# ... work in /tmp ...
popd   # return to previous directory
```

Read lines from stdin into an indexed array.

```bash
mapfile -t lines < file.txt
mapfile -t words < <(echo "a b c" | tr ' ' '\n')
readarray -t arr < file.txt   # alias for mapfile
```

completions.label: readarray
completions.detail: Read lines into an array (alias for mapfile)

documentation.value:
Read lines from stdin into an indexed array. Alias for `mapfile`.

```bash
readarray -t lines < file.txt
```
